html: Our paper Subsurface Vacancy Engineering Enables Atomically Clean and Oxidation-Resistant Copper Interfaces for Anode-Free Lithium Metal Batteries is accepted in ACS Nano. Implanting copper ions into commercial Cu foil removes the native oxide and seeds subsurface vacancies that trap oxygen, keeping the current-collector interface atomically clean and oxidation-resistant; the engineered interface sustains a 98.8% Coulombic efficiency over 600 cycles in anode-free cells.

Our review Artificial Intelligence for Materials Science: Transforming Research Paradigms is out in Chemical Reviews. It surveys how AI is reshaping each stage of materials research, from design and synthesis through characterization to mechanism discovery.

Transforming the synthesis of carbon nanotubes with machine learning models and automation published in Matter. We pair machine-learning models with an automated growth platform to steer carbon-nanotube synthesis, turning manual trial-and-error into a data-driven loop.

Nano-seeding catalysts for wafer-scale horizontally aligned carbon nanotube arrays published in Nature Communications. A nano-seeding catalyst design grows high-density, horizontally aligned carbon-nanotube arrays that stay uniform across a full wafer.
